mission.title: kube-rs: Create a Controller<K> runtime
mission.description: Create a Controller<K> runtime. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-rs deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-rs version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-rs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-rs
helm list -n kube-rs 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kube-rs installation.

steps.title: Review kube-rs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-rs configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-rs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-rs
kubectl get configmap -n kube-rs -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kube-rs
```
To provide something like [controller-runtime](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/controller-runtime/tree/master/examples/crd)'s `Controller`s.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Create a Controller<K> runtime
steps.description:
Sketch of Controller<T>. Heavy WIP for #148 

Some thoughts:
- using a pull based model like  in Informer because it feels nicer than callbacks, and is consistent
- probably will use some kind of signalling to ensure we can debounce events and notify the owner of what does the parallel poll when it should release an item into the stream

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-rs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kube-rs
kubectl get events -n kube-rs --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Create a Controller<K> runtime" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Sketch of Controller<T>. Heavy WIP for #148 

Some thoughts:
- using a pull based model like  in Informer because it feels nicer than callbacks, and is consistent
- probably will use some kind of signalling to ensure we can debounce events and notify the owner of what does the parallel poll when it should release an item into the stream

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-rs installed or the issue environment reproducible.
